latest.title: Glaciers carve mountains into sharp ridges
latest.script_key: look at those knife edge ridges they re not carved by wind or rain it s the glacier s slow grind ice grabs rocks at its base like sandpaper dragging them downhill that s why the peaks stay sharp instead of rounding off see those scrapes on the rocks those are the glacier s signature ever wonder why some valleys look like troughs now you know

latest.title: Lions hunt better when the pride coordinates
latest.script_key: they look calm but one twitch could flip the switch watch a lion s ears each flicks toward the slightest sound that s why they nap like this short light sleeps that keep them tuned in rest saves energy but those ears they re the early warning system ever notice how they pop up first

latest.title: Bees and ladybugs share the same flower
latest.script_key: same flower different day i love this one look how the bee s knees are dusted yellow pollen it ll carry home the ladybug s just grazing on the aphids lurking under the petals that s why neither bugs care about each other because the flower s big enough for both

latest.title: Rivers carry stone grains while they flow
latest.script_key: rivers move tiny pieces of stone downstream watch the water near the rocks because moving current can lift sand roll pebbles and grind larger stones over time the stream is carrying the landscape in small pieces which water clue should we follow next

latest.title: Storm clouds reveal a storm's heat engine
latest.script_key: storm clouds show where a storm is feeding watch the spiral shape because warm ocean air rises near the center and releases heat as clouds build rotation organizes that energy into bands from above the storm is not random it is an engine which satellite clue next

latest.title: Deer aim their ears like sound radar
latest.script_key: deer can aim each ear toward danger watch the ears first because deer can turn them to sample sound from different directions that quick listening check helps them decide whether to freeze flee or keep feeding one quiet second tells the whole body what to do would you freeze

latest.title: Snakes sample the air with a tongue flick
latest.script_key: snakes sample the air with a tongue flick watch the tongue flick because it collects scent particles and sends them to a special organ in the mouth that helps snakes track prey mates and danger without needing a loud chase the air is information would you notice the trail
